diagrams.description: Criminology is the scientific study of crime, its causes, consequences, and societal responses. This flowchart outlines its analytical framework: the ultimate goal is to understand, prevent, and control criminal behavior. It integrates the core aspects of crime (definition, measurement, and types), examines explanatory theories (classical, biological/psychological, sociological, critical), and analyzes the criminal justice system (law enforcement, courts, corrections). These elements are connected through a cyclical process that informs policy, prevention, and intervention strategies, ultimately aiming to reduce crime and enhance public safety.
